# Cyoda

> Cyoda is an open-source entity database management system (EDBMS) and workflow runtime.
> Define entity types, enforce lifecycle transitions, and query temporal history.
> Written in Go. Single binary. SQLite-backed local storage by default.
> Client libraries for Java, Python, Go, and Kotlin via gRPC.
> Apache 2.0 License. No sign-up required.

## Documentation

- [Install and First Entity](https://docs.cyoda.net/getting-started/install-and-first-entity/): Install Cyoda, run it locally, import a workflow, create an entity, transition it, and read state back
- [API Reference](https://docs.cyoda.net/reference/api/): Full API documentation
- [Entity Lifecycle](https://docs.cyoda.net/concepts/entities-and-lifecycle/): How entities and state machines work
- [Configuration](https://docs.cyoda.net/reference/configuration/): Local and production configuration
- [Working with Entities](https://docs.cyoda.net/build/working-with-entities/): End-to-end entity patterns

## Project

- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/Cyoda-platform/cyoda-go): Source code, issues, releases
- [Cyoda Cloud](https://ai.cyoda.net/): Hosted Cyoda
- [Enterprise Cyoda](https://cyoda.com): Enterprise Cyoda
- [Changelog](https://DOMAIN_PLACEHOLDER/changelog): Release history
